textByHash.007b04c72b958afe41ad85c10634089591991ccf: Not much. Just came to see how things are going. Not visited here in a while you see. Me and Roald used to talk all the time.
textByHash.01aaca48f23556d0af0e776ba8c1bd6854b08b26: Yeah, it keeps them happy that way, but don't tell anyone!
textByHash.052640d196f59c95c36b2c4f4f39729811a6c667: I'd like two Mahogany cabinets built.
textByHash.05424b23508862f9fc23990e8050856617281e4c: Yes, but the fish round here are oh so tasty! And there's nobody who can make a good old fashioned seafood gumbo like the Lumbridge cook!
textByHash.06864a3a2a47a60f5683d1018d1f50fc57768338: Okay, I'll join you there. That's going to be a long walk on these paws.
textByHash.072c0aa02211da6204af235cdea73645e349a6b9: I'd like two Wooden bookcases built.
textByHash.0890e8b1013b445a50a252eef63b0aeecb72d5f9: After the battle, myself, Tristan and Camorra split the key into four pieces. We vowed to ensure that the dragonkin would never recover them, and to only return once the war was won.
textByHash.09061b0e8ae47a915bf13540008c008e0e677c86: Well I suppose I don't have anything better to do. Not unless Unferth has forgotten where he lives again.
textByHash.09c517cd2e96175736e971d86d2b11ac1ccfb10b: Me too. Care to swap recipes? I have a rather unique hotdog sauce.
textByHash.0cbaf9771b07dab7f0eca3d7565e1a5fda4cce98: Hello. Shall we go back into the dream again?
textByHash.0d086b79faa868f1855bf8530c10cd682b332971: No, it's just that there comes a time in every cats life where he realises how much time he has wasted just.... playing with rats.
textByHash.0ed31e01e4fdfcedb3c4454993d97f766bbae85b: You know, cats are the first species to fully evolve. We're perfect.
textByHash.1172a3f3b766eebff9ee42342ab375fa1c676e3d: Anything not nailed down is a cat toy.
textByHash.12ae6fca0caa96929affbc082f5c39c2ab762252: would say, she's the cat's whiskers. Oh how I long to be curled up with her in front of a log fire and a big bowl of tuna. Menaphos would be a great place to start a family.
textByHash.15096d19cf381872679360b923170f16a9ae96f1: I am drawing a picture of that thing over there.
textByHash.152688eb24f8bcffc4abd7bcda25d462bdbbc2a4: If a player falls down in a forest and nobody is there, do they make any noise?
textByHash.1577ee3eb11f9784fa026f5a3216770d6d6213ed: Ah, another person who thinks I want to talk to them just because they can understand me.
textByHash.163e0862eb7eabfd44701efbf464d5fc40654a8e: Last night I was in the kitchen and I heard a noise from the cellar. I opened the trapdoor and saw a creature dart into a hole in the wall.
textByHash.166fd6468ce37f710a95bb608ea8b3172c2ea223: But I don't have any memories of Robert the Strong. It was only when the Sphinx hypnotised me that we learnt who I really was and I don't even remember that.
textByHash.17bcd3aa5a2016db878d454528daa0f9478a42d5: I'll give you a list of what needs doing.
textByHash.1a17f183b3c18c052082de49c721ab5abba75e18: Yikes!! Where did you pop up from?
textByHash.1cc05f4397ab362d9f1106c1fc8116f3d9f419b0: Oh, well... what do you want?
textByHash.1df5876e74b479fc626c62c1dc95e015a02e1a81: The scars of our war with the dragonkin remain to this day, and the weapon in that vault could still be very dangerous.
textByHash.1e546196d318e1ba1022819dcbbce284567618f1: Good stuff. Light the brazier then add the potion.
textByHash.1f43b0001b89a47b895685791ecc66ed062a8d5e: Yes! I buy and sell axes! Take your pick (or axe)!
textByHash.1faed7b75a1f3ab9331d72159115742dc18884c3: Cat Rule number 3: If you ever make a mistake, which is rare and probably involves a dog, make sure you wash yourself immediately. This will give everyone the impression that
textByHash.207c124e53d032818572356d1d79b54df06a56e3: What a beauty she is! It's the way the shimmer from the hot sand makes her eyes sparkle, the way she flicks her tail as she leaps gracefully across the pyramids, the way she cleans herself after a good rat. As you humans
textByHash.20f57d6c75690471bbd5ffec5a4956171434f009: In this place it's not HOW you die that matters. It's who you take with you.
textByHash.218b8e372df3c18fca417a85bbecc4ec58994008: All I know is that Gertrude found me on her doorstep one day. She looked after me when I was a kitten.
textByHash.21f617daee4835c3a4de1a4a1504a99e2f78d201: Sorry, I am far too busy marking my territory.
textByHash.2344413b23cb170bfe1f7625e1b13505e9885704: I'd like a Teak wardrobe built.
textByHash.236ee5bc34923c39bd3550ef1ac2fc64fa7852ba: So, you want to know how us cats keep so clean all day?
textByHash.2638d1bfeda3fb03fe156033614f66e72703c0ca: Myself and Neite are going away for a few days. Could you look after Unferth for me whilst I'm away?
textByHash.27ea535d8863c01aa23de383592f4a6f5feb66d8: Those who live by the sword, die by the mage.
textByHash.27fd5f0bb87899cdc29cff7d6a9fbb6518324a74: Probably. It's just no challenge these days.
textByHash.2b0da8026bda22cb9e270e6d651f20da081368ca: No way. I am here catching mice.
textByHash.2b53edc588bc67b26edfa4c413cd46d0d24d52e4: No problem. Just make sure you bring me some of you ever get the chance to make it. I love that gumbo!
textByHash.2b957ee6a3f023e042b2f9bebc41e59e3eeea4f5: Apparently you need to defeat the guardian on the other side of this barrier. Sounds odd to me, but what do I know.
textByHash.2c231d1d1a22a25a5772566f1b810892707743cd: Well that all sounds great to me. I'll meet you in Sophanem.
textByHash.2d7e1b18320f4aa1f54040e2629289e60b4c218a: Oh yes, an old recipe passed down from generation to generation of cooks. But this might be the last generation since the Duke's current cook doesn't have any children.
textByHash.2e0f10231472242eaf58ebb2051f59bd188a0205: So Not Bob, why are you here?
textByHash.2ee3daf268b93677cc5adf22acf2b37f7d48a460: I'm not ready to set out yet. You go on ahead. I'll meet you on Lunar Isle and we'll ask the Oneiromancer to help us.
textByHash.327eeddbb00f301e30ca453f539edd1be499601f: Noooooo. Now that mouse I was chasing has got away. Can you be a bit more sneaky next time? You know, sneaky sneaky stick da chompy as my old friend Rantz often says.
textByHash.32dcfd5801a75c8f1403d710b8bea1e0a43102d0: I'd like a Teak drawer built.
textByHash.340ad6b59b742922c450e8305fdb53d4b1d8693b: OK, you asked what I'm doing here.
textByHash.35c123784c5c7043800453b55a2ab1524614c5c3: You should get to work on making that potion.
textByHash.39fc6d2a0719e193eec22a2a5b8eb87f8f83b727: Well, I don't believe much really since I know I am Zar... erm, well, no, I erm, meow?
textByHash.3d199e98dfef6f7cf3342cdf8e83fb1fabd20879: You don't have anything I can repair.
textByHash.432d516d1f1b908153db09301247046fba852ae5: The tunnel was too dark for me to follow it, so I went to tell the Duke. But when we went down to the cellar the hole had been blocked up, and no one believes me.
textByHash.452dadd6172e60b0016686c9662a03761eb166da: Yeh, the old King and I go way back.
textByHash.487189cd2cca23c293d3b369bfb86cd0ded520ca: Erm, hello? Can you understand me?
textByHash.4b303ba6fe597d7c5959325f96c1e31a63fc068e: I'd like two Teak bookcases built.
textByHash.4d1374e12ba248f1539843613dc5af05d9fd477f: I hid one of my pieces in the most unlikely of places, an abandoned dragonkin fortress. You'll find the fortress on a snow covered island, north west of Rellekka.
textByHash.4d40c67b6cb40d0e9b5b7c6757a3ca99d6a69d66: With the dragonkin long gone, the vault should be safe to open. That said, you should still be cautious.
textByHash.4ecf9f1efe73cc1f1a3f4257844b2f65e0c493cf: It won't, but I have faith in you. Now you'd better head to the fleet. I'll see you in Rellekka.
textByHash.4f4495168e7043416337b2b862d08935eceb2d95: So you buy this Robert the Strong stuff?
textByHash.562936d8feab80b4754c48fd2e17a7640dee83c6: Advice you want eh? Well, I could just enlighten you with a snippet of my vast knowledge.
textByHash.5665231dc3959216ba17ffbd96635b66e5cd0a91: It looks like we may have a slight problem on our hands.
textByHash.57f30b13fd7345918feb671849fe9c2cc47b44ee: An Advanced Combat Training Device, every cat should have one. Here's another one.
textByHash.595d27f16629adbe4cee689119945026657e65d1: That gullible thing over there. Next to that tree where that man sometimes stands.
textByHash.609c276e75e521f6d6c707ad9c8084eb19882778: Apparently so yes. Well, that's what they told me after the experiment.
textByHash.60c03b99c7ff5104e2e06b201e208b50ddfbc711: Thank you! If you can convince the Duke I'm telling the truth then we can get to the bottom of this mystery.
textByHash.615ef26d60aedc68009594133f4b748051bdfa35: Ha! How old indeed. How old are the trees? How old are the waves that crash against the shore? How old is the stone we walk on?
textByHash.616dfc85193ecf56c16452c4ef2296b839c27b35: Butchered!!! I think not. Cats are easily as tough as sheep. Cats were the second thing Guthix brought here, after sheep of course.
textByHash.61ae50ddf2849edd02a71106a691eb0506fca877: I'm here to study the druids. They're boring, but they know how to treat animals, and they don't go around bothering you just because they've got a silly gadget that lets them understand what you say!
textByHash.632518b90931873270055d96ecb9f05d186f924a: Could I have a word with you outside? There's something we need to discuss.
textByHash.63fa9d9dd85d90ae9ce64ed2f7ce24351891066d: So all we need to do is go through the barrier?
textByHash.653fc0d7b098238fde904f3123e58a4a4407750e: We'll have to worry about that later. For now, you'll need to take out these dragons.
textByHash.6598488e5f475590a3ba4d16809a399cfb06d871: I'd like a Wooden wardrobe built.
textByHash.6669ac54fe74dc6c08baa0645c403870693de165: That's right. Before we get into that though, about that thing I mentioned...
textByHash.6770474b0e71d2edd467013a07f81605356e8617: Ah. I should've realised Unferth would miss me.
textByHash.6805ff589dddea138e1a0cc237e908d2629a9102: Yes and no. I now have my old memories again, but there are so many of them. I can't quite piece them all together yet. I need time to properly work things out.
textByHash.6819504a082c26ed56b95443254e0bb2a958a1cd: Cats and teleporting just don't mix well. That's why I walk everywhere. Messes my hair up terribly.
textByHash.691ec40cb6b79b3b891b6fa8747284ce65708e53: I hope you're right. You'd better head in and get this meeting done. Come talk to me once it's finished. There's something we need to discuss.
textByHash.6a116384b39180842fa62f7a2a4e6cd879e14344: Argh! My thumbs have gone weird!
textByHash.6a68079c0d19d629efbce96affe9ffd377e697f4: Hello. We need to talk about Zorgoth.
textByHash.6b94395a17d671ec34c5e0ece0bbcc644d308034: And don't come back, you filthy goblin!
textByHash.6c9cfd66830c3fdb8bda7bf1e15849e0ac906cd0: Now that I've started to regain my memories, I find myself hungry for answers. I'd like to see for myself what these doors guard.
textByHash.6d67627194a840957ba96312af05a514022ebf33: Yes. There was this professor called 'Oddenstein' I think. He must have tricked me into that machine of his, then the next thing I knew I was feline!
textByHash.6d71449b7b8ad2007e0f6e5982d93cac60ebd34b: Actually no. I come from round here originally. Back then all this was just fields.
textByHash.6fa4783759bfe13347ac865e9d320785641d3879: No thanks. I'm on my way back now.
textByHash.7004bc9d3ca40c7bbeae3c96b2ee902bc2b4e91b: Well, it's more to do with what happens when you hear that being said. You see, something interesting happens somewhere else.
textByHash.708e1a2484096f9577ec10d9ee068c802494c8d2: I'd like a Wooden drawer built.
textByHash.711cddfa8226c0e48c2c8990106c8e2187f9ac54: If only you knew the power of Bob. Your master never told you who your real father was, did he?
textByHash.75af6749388164014847087da2544d240827e359: Hmmm, yes, that looks pretty badly damaged. Unfortunately, I don't have the tools to fix it, but Brian over in Port Sarim might be able to.
textByHash.7811ab7345c38fdbfbb95b3cc232143477bef1f5: Apparently you aren't as stupid as you look.
textByHash.7ad799bd7d269df9e6fe6142b8e28dd6629cdb02: , why do you keep your cat in the bank?
textByHash.7ae6b665de2d674943444b461954e6cf7d269345: I guess that depends what it is.
textByHash.7ca192c64b009c8a63e7479793d1c41c7a509dba: I'd like two Oak cabinets built.
textByHash.7d1386bebdc10c2a05473a8595b3901a01ec28c2: I am. I can't fight any more, but I know a lot about the dragonkin. I might be able to use that knowledge to help. Besides, much of this is my fault, so it's only right that I help fix it. Now let's get going.
textByHash.7d521db475fb2114fd362ccad6cb00174240d073: Ah, don't mind me. I am learning a second language!
textByHash.7deca63abe201199066175a941e309f8225b90a0: I haven't been home because I've wanted to be alone.
textByHash.7e444e16580772f28bb494959a569385da96ac07: Good stuff. Add the potion to the brazier and we can begin.
textByHash.7e6a5bd128944d8d6be16cf662389ea4e6f7c630: Maybe not, but I'm right where I need to be.
textByHash.7f0b6ce7d59802e28723fc460fa03a0423f09036: I'd like a Oak drawer built.
textByHash.808212b276f90f1685696ed7848c798ec388d37d: This isn't right... We've made a mistake. We need to leave now!
textByHash.80d592ce2578f7f87eb92db9d827bd5b2948681d: What dogs? I have already rid this place of dogs. Have you ever seen one?
textByHash.8127e36a4ebdb13e432601272f21a7cbc2095584: Are you trying to be funny?
textByHash.81bcaf8134a0cff1fff49541d26146257118c464: Cats are rather delicate creatures and they are subject to a good many ailments, but I never heard of one who suffered from insomnia.
textByHash.83fd231eee7ed4bb06b9fcc2211860e71133a09d: Hey! I'm just this cat, you know?
textByHash.8588572daf1ab2390a4cff5abaaeae9ef8bc2841: Cat Rule number 4: Beware the bag monster. These things live in small bags and under newspapers, sometimes even hidden in shoes if they are very clever. They will be very small and
textByHash.860a86859a93c8d562da61bcb69c8f4e754c5547: I'd like two Oak bookcases built.
textByHash.861afc8ed6b5abba5c37ae37f53ae6ce8568d726: take on the same colour as the hiding place. Attack these things on sight - stop at nothing. If you see a bag or shoe you have not inspected recently, pounce!!
textByHash.864f2b4bfce18b13a97c6b8c84176ebb7f25155a: Please excuse me, I'm rather busy.
textByHash.891203ea7bba90bab4653331496ec5d40c7ac685: All sorts of stuff! I pick up no end of comments on my travels. Want to hear one?
textByHash.8b3676aa138edf89b8ef3c39c2893de386989d09: Let's see if it works then.
textByHash.8b9e397246a9e8f511b67b80c53d0032aea9b651: Oh, I wouldn't do that if I were you, he's very sensitive about his gumbo responsibilities. I'm sure that if you're a good enough fisherman and cook the knowledge will reach you one day.
textByHash.8be44a202dc8cdd6e20fb4c8fef8299e517be27a: No. In the end, we went our seperate ways. Of the four pieces, Camorra took one, Tristan took another and I took the final two.
textByHash.8dc22a8a41d33b6b91eda92a5f7fdf0014b33bd7: No, not really. I'm actually signed up with all those HAM fanatics. Kill the whole lot of 'em!!
textByHash.931c62cdc64fa10be3534a5b8f231c7091c2fae1: The final piece I took to Karamja. I hid it in an ancient temple on the south coast of the island.
textByHash.935de568e276c0ba552b743d55834f7e55f34521: Yes I guess so. But I've been asked to get some coal for Dunstan. He ran out, you see.
textByHash.95083f2e30d7863b50c85e2723bbb2f67d8145da: No, not really. I've just had a severe disappointment.
textByHash.973079cd823246cc7ff5d828701d3567c10dd3aa: Get out of my shop! GET OUT!
textByHash.983c5ef0bca3be9acc55a12b84e901825500710c: Defeat the guardian? I'm no good at combat... I'm a cat.
textByHash.988d3e8f75ff5cab1cb9de706bcf825ed3141476: Sigh... OK. Did you hear about the cat who drank 10 bowls of milk? He set a new lap record.
textByHash.99a43782912c71aaa6864beb9cf4e898add65b64: I've got a lot to take in and understand, but I'm okay.
textByHash.9aeac939195ac4bebb7a2b47b01f4c0b244528ef: Thank you so much! Would you like a cup of tea before you go?
textByHash.9ccef788cbb3a7b6774de79230663a72def336bb: Now, where did the rest of my PK clan go?
textByHash.9d90049d723c67b9473a587c8a09c82296122f8b: No, I know where I'm going.
textByHash.9eea84fc26b24e659629ebc630d5ec7d993c2878: Good stuff. Take it to the brazier in the middle of town and we can begin the dream.
textByHash.9f3112510ddedaa5b1bba404e2dd39b4c7eae432: Well, I think I will let you decide that.
textByHash.a02b4df7f4a60c5ee53187184a188e286c88fce8: Cat Rule number 2: When assisting a human at work, make sure you sit behind its right leg. You stand a much better chance of getting picked up and stroked. Also, it's very amusing
textByHash.a1067960fd04f00db76a3b4e89cd3467a282dab1: Cats are smarter than dogs. You can't get eight cats to pull a sled through snow.
textByHash.a1cbea17549795864febb9a77984f4280a732615: I'd like a Mahogany wardrobe built.
textByHash.a1f8790ad6dff45fadb7e9e24ca2cf9f749f26f5: Oh fudge. Give me one moment.
textByHash.a24346d8c5c410671c44e5ee7b15bb9ca76d6140: Never question the logic of a cat, however flawed.
textByHash.a276a68e577c0885c25667dd9c7e0bf0b8c7c650: Yes. Without Galvek, Zorgoth has nothing. Removing his army is the only chance of stopping him. It must be done, no matter the cost.
textByHash.a3301198d6c8961bbf16976877fb35d34e9d2e5b: We don't serve the likes of that thing in here.
textByHash.a424811fd83f0f55a30deb4b9626ce636d2f36b3: Did you find out who my parents are?
textByHash.a50bd968bd8db5211c30c5128d00649fcb95578c: I'd like two Mahogany bookcases built.
textByHash.a5569239f7a19c060ffe64d26fa0090125544765: Care to place a wager on that? Sorry, hang on, I've got no pockets. Maybe next time.
textByHash.a57193b807f633385e3a59f06c9a0a7356bcde43: Sigh. I can't believe it but I've fallen for Neite!
textByHash.a5bf246be8438a5cced7135d9a103ea2d0d106d5: I'd like a Mahogany drawer built.
textByHash.a6cd885411c7de8c3f7a2cb9024e8ec9664eb6db: I'd like a Teak table built.
textByHash.a879841d726e9fd0e2d1b93a904e03ad80b06ffc: Grrrowl. OK, last try. What do you call a cat that lives in an igloo?.... An Eskimew!
textByHash.a8a7639ae7fe3c23a583cedd0b6d260fa76bb2fa: I am walking towards the tower...
textByHash.aa010fab790700b930257a3aec9fc46cce0f30a0: Yeah, that's one of my best qualities. Just behind sneaky and vicious. Besides, after a few doses of catnip, they all look like mice to me!
textByHash.aa7272f4349c1269aa7c705db41ef1e3135be5e3: There you go, happy doing business with you!
textByHash.ab1b63f1c7ebecb69853b782ac43b921f3f32b97: Tricks? Dogs are for tricks! Cats are for admiring and fussing over.
textByHash.ab91beebb442fddd257fa32ece754c5f15b877c3: Hey , could you do me a favour?
textByHash.ac4c752583371530a449debbea52bd128e3e2ee9: I was eating a particularly nice piece of tuna.
textByHash.add2b076f44a613c28bedb2cc09b7ef8fe85866e: I don't really care as long as no one thinks it's me. Maybe it was that strange poison seller who headed towards the seers village.
textByHash.b055dfe1d53f0e8117b2dd9008aa6dedb6b3efb0: So you and the Sphinx tell me.
textByHash.b1b1fa762583ad58acea0e1a2f9acd03f3ad7c34: Oh, sorry... I meant, erm, meow?
textByHash.b7f63892d6706d5214ada390518d77192b24c9de: No, I just know what I like. Besides, it was a joke.
textByHash.b903ce530b5de2c622c7c588c0915f2606050332: Sorry, no time for chat. Still got mouse bones stuck in my teeth. How awkward.
textByHash.b9b057e67f414de58515a672eacf9913589b55d1: Hello. I'm on my way to Sophanem to see the Sphinx.
textByHash.bd9021a6716f45fa92cbde02235f2cd2aea2d44c: I'd like two Teak cabinets built.
textByHash.becb2b915d3d625e944a5fdb7dbb670dc2cfe3a8: Don't get distracted so easily! See, I'm distracting you right now!
textByHash.c08fea63095c86cebc39c0c3ccc58e38df5f26ef: Dragonkin are not easy to kill. In fact, they are immune to most weapons. But Zorgoth made it clear that Galvek is the key to his plan. The only reason he has this army is because of that dragon.
textByHash.c0ec8365e3b906f2c05e2719d89e6160e73f7cc2: We were defending ourselves. You threatened to destroy us all.
textByHash.c1ca941e88b94caafce96aec38b8bbfca665342e: You can destroy Zamorak. He has foreseen this. It is your destiny. Join me, and together we can rule this place as father and son.
textByHash.c3a7ba776d98c69e03b5eb5fd3e85952486ab7b0: Be wery, wery quiet... I'm hunting pwayers.
textByHash.c693bcf126a61df66bc82fe22872b269db04527d: I told you about the creature I saw. You should go and tell the Duke.
textByHash.c7d6a9a451cfda3fa464b5c1c04c023749a8db39: However, one thing that I do remember clearly is that battle. I also remember what happened next.
textByHash.c910cf6d6e9d5ab6f2878c4d0a054a582d7e0e85: Hello again. The Oneiromancer and I were just discussing what our perfect Sunday would be.
textByHash.c9d59659ffa669f08e044d6449b3250cfe10afef: I know but there is something about her. The way she...well, it's everything! Oh, the hidden pain of love. Love lies not with the eyes but with the mind, and therefore is the winged Cupid painted blind.
textByHash.ca4181071133d3b6938b2fe715a2a2650e8810f7: Wonderful! Neite and I have been all over Gielinor! We 'borrowed' a magic carpet...
textByHash.ca88550061cc3f3f39d71ee40546b4071972f575: Have you been to the Sphinx yet?
textByHash.cae053a1cbfc0892659ef745bd6adcc866afaa3c: Mate! You need a new line of work!
textByHash.caeaacd7f0e1ebd366ca8042efeb030b180c3108: Ah, don't mind me. I'm just looting bodies.
textByHash.cc990211737a6648083f577d5863adb0a1106035: Sorry friend, but I can't do anything with that.
textByHash.ccfa1d01aebf235770ccc62022cff4972b63e229: If you absolutely must know, we had a problem with the beehive in the garden, and as all of our servants are so pathetically useless, I decided I would deal with it myself. So I did.
textByHash.ce0dc4be00ffcb5d568795a7f1e66a591af8d452: Yeah, I could tell you something really useful, but then I would have to '1-hit you' to death.
textByHash.ce12cf50921dfd5b8369474366526ee3d8f9dfe1: I suppose I had better talk to you then.
textByHash.ce38a5d247997cadc5b1829f1900d7bd0edde4c7: But we killed them before they could leave.
textByHash.d0bf7fca98207923f8e44bbdce40ab294964132e: Look out!! He's over there!! Run!!
textByHash.d2047e9eda630f9ac7a12d022f37542dc310a5a7: Ahh, I guess your right. Thanks!
textByHash.d3475e43b258e07149571b859f4c36496cd2fffe: I think not. They are my minions, and I let him think that he's in charge. I'm the real power behind the throne.
textByHash.d3c6dee2708513b446d979915e840510feeac7cf: Nothing! The furthest back I can remember is Gertrude looking after me.
textByHash.d450e45d42987016030cf3500c10e7b7f6601da4: Dogs believe they are human. Cats believe they are Guthix!
textByHash.d4f702bc2e12bb85184fa84a18f172f75ff5b179: Perhaps one day you will learn the truth. Until then, the Oddenstein story is what we will tell the 'others'. Understood?
textByHash.d59cf0e7bc3c1d8078e8a56815068d630bd66ac3: I was walking by myself in the garden.
textByHash.d5edd5f430b8b3f505758333570bec8ccab050fc: I'd like a Mahogany table built.
textByHash.d6f4a4bfe1d655158197f2e1c79fd26b7201aef7: I remember! Lithkren! The dragonkin! The vault!
textByHash.d902762340a7a5a10e6d6b70372848120dc76b63: Thank goodness you're here. We've got ourselves into quite the situation.
textByHash.d99787c9545d56770e4b31c2ddcfc1fd6ced7000: The key was split into four different pieces. Camorra took one piece, Tristan took another and I took the final two.
textByHash.d9d022b62b0ce94389a697d0bcf8b12f364ac1ac: You know, sometimes I wonder why I still bother with this place. It's just not the same since the humans arrived.
textByHash.dbd1fa1f9564295aa8d0b815fa0bb56864d70484: Yes you do. Dive deep, search your feelings.
textByHash.dbfb51197e536748298a44ab822d93a747e35ba2: It's not the dragons I'm worried about. It's Zorgoth.
textByHash.dc6f0718218f60c9a05f7eb9c21d9af1ed9aac1a: There are many intelligent species in the universe. They are all owned by cats.
textByHash.dd889c11b8b0544c57d6591fe466c15f39c16429: It looked a bit like a goblin, but it had big bulging eyes. It wasn't wearing armour, but it had this odd helmet with a light on it.
textByHash.dda417deaa8759d044ff9ba6fbd30152a3049a3f: Camorra eventually boarded a ship for lands to the far west, never to be seen again. She took her key piece with her.
textByHash.df35083c5a34d9b3a154f31d66916b649a738729: I am in front of a dark tower... the cat is called Odysseus.
textByHash.e24b22d4269d6e7794eed6f38e8618f5b824fb99: Hmm... Wish I had a cat to talk to.
textByHash.e25d9b28a4c85333299749cba53ad95fa654c7ec: I'd like a Wooden table built.
textByHash.e2601862c9c30576b8565bfe46211fa5b04c7869: Sorry, I'm far too busy calculating thermal vectors.
textByHash.e2b5279d02ff5e88c8e1cf5f587075eeafd7831e: Ah so you've met some of the local felines. Wise and confusing, those Sphinges.
textByHash.e33eee8857a4eb2b11367493713075aecaf43c7a: You would think so wouldn't you. But is being covered in spit really clean?
textByHash.e39798371f18c934941b7a6e5a21f786ef2fda69: If at first you don't succeed, take a short nap.
textByHash.e3c34b652d1dab82fb96187e6fe06107f7308e6b: No time to talk. Get out there and kill those dragons.
textByHash.e458f9d1c52acc96a62f9dd5ff53d25a6058426e: Well, those white knights are far more complicated than you might imagine. Not the 'goodie goodies' they claim to be. Just watch your back.
textByHash.e7130ab44df704df1855d682abb29e902ac82fd8: Right!! Fellow cats, prepare for war!!
textByHash.e7d543d0c541c1d7394340d3c995547d12565905: He is the real threat here. You can kill as many dragons as you want in this battle, but it won't be over until Zorgoth himself is dealt with.
textByHash.e8027d0032ffc158657e6e06306c31ea9a2d4bea: Tristan passed his piece on to his descendants. They lived in the city of Saranthium, where Varrock now stands. Perhaps the Varrock Library has some record of them.
textByHash.e919f23da5cf16dc1adc1140e60a7464837a10eb: You know, my feet are knackered. This walking lark isn't all it's cracked up to be. Give me a nice monitor to sleep on any day.
textByHash.e9cb79b92fefae2af5d7529db0165e612eab5142: Did you see that? Someone with a big shiny new halberd hiding behind that rock. I thought only rangers acted as cowardly as that.
textByHash.ea3ef628abafae4b8a6e6e388e41f5056d6a99ef: Exactly!! Clearly I must go home, the sky's beginning to bruise, night must fall and I shall be forced to camp.
textByHash.ea6821d1487b721daefd7e33631a9bf6bddc4b6d: Of course I'll repair it, though the materials may cost you. Just hand me the item and I'll have a look.
textByHash.eeb5b3270a87a982c52a7d711cf1db99daf6a986: I'd like a Oak table built.
textByHash.f0eb49823443d4650919fd88aa7bd5fe8a395348: I'd like two Wooden cabinets built.
textByHash.f0fdc923efe7e11d6ac0c0af8c1b7fdf030ac41c: Dogs have owners, cats have staff.
textByHash.f1024e97cc583fbea26b93f50dfe45bee3260ecb: Ahh, the human shows signs of a brain. Why do you think that is?
textByHash.f16be68dc5a02436815e402a4f39f29d382bd06a: She said she has feelings for me but would never get involved with someone like me that has no family or status.
textByHash.f50087262dbfaa847924cbc490f88587dad58d24: It's some thread. great clue. No, really.
textByHash.f57638344e41a59d704bcc5443b267d6fd7f55e6: Yes. Once the authorities found out about his experiments he ran away. Lucky for him or I would scratch his eyes out.
textByHash.f62f82838955727e2148f458c5adaf1e65e46933: Camorra boarded a ship for lands to the far west, never to be seen again. She took her key piece with her.
textByHash.f7857af45fa46319d0d88b7fd6204a6d81e281ac: Cat Rule number 1: Always act afraid when near dogs, especially if you're near a human. They will pick you up and fuss you, then shout at the dog. Most amusing!
textByHash.f78f58d570e931fdeaaf2aef580b3967bd822f01: Okay... so who are you then?
textByHash.f7944e6db90f96a96d0cae2c61fcfcbf78e1d786: I'd like a Oak wardrobe built.
textByHash.f7eb26973fae54d3608522d98c117d20c41e004c: Ahh, but it is you see. It does all sorts of wonderous things.
textByHash.fbf20f5964386f37d67ad2759e0b1049f577be62: Hmph...Oh Neite...My bounty is as boundless as the sea, my love as deep. The more I give to thee, the more I have, for both are infinite.
textByHash.fbfe2b60e935385366e7074c8063b77a58784ca3: I have studied many philosophers and many cats. The wisdom of cats is infinitely superior.
